heroSubtitle: Surajkund sits at a practical crossroads of Faridabad, Delhi, and the wider NCR corridor, which means families here have more tutor options than most parts of Haryana — but also more complexity in choosing the right fit. IB Gram helps you navigate this by matching your child to tutors suited to their programme, subject, level, and school calendar. Availability depends on subject, schedule, exact location, tutor fit, and mode.
introSummary: Students near Surajkund attend schools spread across the Green Fields and Neharpar corridor, with easy access to the Delhi border via Shooting Range Road. IB Gram connects these families with experienced IB tutors for PYP, MYP, and DP — at home, online, or in hybrid sessions that flex around school timetables and internal assessment deadlines.

contentBlocks.heading: Why Surajkund Families Seek Specialist IB Support
contentBlocks.body: Surajkund occupies a distinctive position within the NCR education belt. The area is close to the Delhi border near Badarpur and Shooting Range Road, which means families here often have children enrolled in international-curriculum schools that draw from a wide catchment — from central Faridabad sectors to Greater Faridabad townships and the Charmwood Village pocket. That geographic spread creates a challenge: the IB curriculum expects independent analytical thinking, not rote preparation, and most schools in this corridor move through the programme at pace. When a student reaches a genuinely difficult concept in Math Analysis and Approaches HL, or when DP2 Internal Assessments begin stacking up alongside school tests and the Extended Essay, parents often discover that a general-subject tutor is not adequate. IB pedagogy — command terms, criterion-referenced marking, the Theory of Knowledge framework — requires someone who has actually taught or studied within the IB system. That specific experience is precisely what IB Gram tries to source for students in Surajkund and the surrounding Faridabad sectors.
items: IB DP students in Surajkund balancing IA deadlines and school tests simultaneously
MYP learners needing criterion-based feedback rather than marks-only correction
PYP families looking for inquiry-led support that matches classroom methodology
Students near the Delhi border who need tutors accessible from both Faridabad and South Delhi

contentBlocks.heading: IB Programmes Covered: PYP, MYP and DP
contentBlocks.body: IB Gram supports students across all three main IB programmes, and the approach differs significantly between them. In the Primary Years Programme, the emphasis is on inquiry-based learning and transdisciplinary thinking; a good PYP tutor encourages questions rather than drilling answers, and understands how Units of Inquiry are structured across grade levels. In the Middle Years Programme, subject-specific criteria become central — students are assessed on strands such as Knowing and Understanding, Investigating Patterns, and Communicating, and tutors who understand these criteria can give feedback that is genuinely actionable rather than generic. In the Diploma Programme, the demands intensify considerably. HL subjects typically require 240 teaching hours, and the internal assessment components — the Math Exploration (IA), the Group 4 project, the Economics IA commentaries, the Language A Individual Oral — carry meaningful weight in the final grade. Additionally, the Extended Essay and TOK Exhibition or Essay require sustained independent thinking with very specific formatting and citation conventions. School deadlines for these components vary by institution, and experienced DP tutors are aware that missing an internal submission date has consequences that extra revision cannot fix. IB Gram tries to match students with tutors who know the DP calendar and can help pace work accordingly.
items: PYP Units of Inquiry and transdisciplinary learning support
MYP criterion-referenced feedback across all assessment strands
DP HL and SL subject preparation with IA and EE guidance
TOK Essay and Exhibition structuring within academic integrity boundaries

contentBlocks.heading: Subject Coverage for Surajkund Students
contentBlocks.body: The subjects that come up most frequently among DP students in the Faridabad area include Mathematics — both the Analysis and Approaches and the Applications and Interpretation pathways — at both HL and SL. Math AA HL is a particularly common request because it is challenging even for strong students, and the internal exploration component requires a level of mathematical writing that most school curricula do not specifically train. Physics HL and Chemistry HL are similarly requested, especially for students targeting science and engineering programmes at universities in India and abroad. Economics is popular among students at international-leaning schools in the NCR corridor, and the three commentary IAs require students to apply real news events to specific economic models — a skill that benefits from structured guidance. Biology HL is sought by students preparing for medicine-related degree programmes. English A — whether Language and Literature or Literature — requires close reading and literary analysis at a standard that catches many students off guard in DP1. IB Gram also supports MYP students in Mathematics, Sciences, Language and Literature, and Individuals and Societies, as well as PYP learners who need targeted numeracy or literacy reinforcement alongside their school programme.
items: Math AA HL and AI HL with IA exploration support
Physics, Chemistry and Biology HL for science-track students
Economics IA commentary structuring and model application
English A close-reading and individual oral preparation

contentBlocks.heading: Home Tutoring Logistics near Surajkund
contentBlocks.body: Surajkund's position near the Delhi border is both an asset and a practical consideration for home tutoring. On the Faridabad side, tutors can reasonably travel to Surajkund from Sector 15, Sector 16, Sector 21, Sector 28, and Sector 31, as well as from Green Fields and Neharpar. From the Delhi side, tutors based in Badarpur, Tughlagabad, or South-East Delhi can often reach Surajkund in under 30 minutes outside peak traffic — though peak-hour congestion on the Mathura Road and Shooting Range Road corridor can add significant time. This means scheduling afternoon or early-evening sessions needs to account for school dismissal times and traffic patterns. For families in Charmwood Village or Greater Faridabad, the distance to a Surajkund-based tutor may be manageable, but for students in Ballabhgarh, online or hybrid tutoring is often a more practical solution than seeking a home tutor who travels that far. Online sessions remove the commute variable entirely, and many IB students — particularly in DP2 when the workload is highest — find that a focused 60-75 minute video session is more efficient than the time lost to travel. IB Gram supports all three modes: home visits within a practical radius, fully online sessions, and hybrid arrangements where in-person and online alternate based on the week.
items: Tutor travel possible from Sectors 15, 16, 21, 28 and Green Fields
South-East Delhi tutors accessible via Badarpur Border in off-peak hours
Online sessions recommended for Ballabhgarh and Greater Faridabad students
Hybrid scheduling to flex around DP2 workload and school test cycles

contentBlocks.heading: How IB Gram Matches Tutors for Surajkund Students
contentBlocks.body: Finding an IB tutor is not the same as finding a tutor for board exams. The IB has its own vocabulary, its own assessment logic, and its own internal submission timelines, and a tutor who is familiar with CBSE or even A-Levels may not be equipped to help a student write a valid Math IA or structure a TOK Essay. IB Gram's matching process begins with understanding the specifics: which programme and year the student is in, which subjects and levels (HL or SL) are causing difficulty, what the school's internal submission schedule looks like, whether the student needs conceptual catch-up or targeted IA or exam revision, and what mode — home, online, or hybrid — works logistically for the family. We also look at tutor communication style and availability alignment, because the best-qualified tutor is not a good match if their schedule conflicts with the student's school calendar or if the teaching style does not suit the learner. For Surajkund families, we also factor in geography and realistic travel time so that a tutor who agrees to home sessions can genuinely commit to the location. The process is a conversation, not a keyword search.
items: Programme, year group, subject and level assessed before matching
School internal submission timeline considered from the start
Tutor travel radius and availability verified for Surajkund location
Communication style and learning-fit evaluated alongside qualifications

contentBlocks.heading: Home, Online and Hybrid: Choosing the Right Mode
contentBlocks.body: Each tutoring mode has genuine advantages depending on the student's age, the subject, and where they live in the Surajkund area. Home tutoring offers the most natural environment for younger PYP and MYP students who benefit from face-to-face interaction and who may find video calls less engaging for extended sessions. It also allows a tutor to review physical notes, textbooks, and school materials directly, which can be useful for MYP criterion feedback on actual drafts. However, home tutoring in Surajkund depends on a tutor being willing and able to travel there consistently — and given traffic patterns around Shooting Range Road and the Badarpur junction, this is not always reliable across a full academic year. Online tutoring eliminates that variable. For DP students working through a Math IA draft, an Economics commentary, or TOK preparation, screen-sharing and document collaboration tools make online sessions highly effective. Hybrid arrangements — where the tutor visits for a fortnightly or monthly check-in and the rest of the sessions are online — are increasingly common among DP2 students who want the relationship-building of in-person contact without losing consistency to traffic or weather disruptions. IB Gram can structure arrangements for any of these three modes based on what the family and tutor both find workable.
items: Home sessions best for younger PYP and MYP students needing face-to-face learning
Online sessions most reliable for DP2 students with intensive IA and exam schedules
Hybrid model balances consistency with the personal connection of in-person visits
Mode choice informed by student age, subject, and Surajkund travel practicality

contentBlocks.heading: How Tutors Are Reviewed and How Parents Stay Informed
contentBlocks.body: Parents in the Surajkund area, particularly those whose children are in DP, are often making a significant time and financial commitment when engaging a private IB tutor, and they reasonably want to know that the person teaching their child is genuinely qualified. IB Gram reviews tutors on the basis of their academic and teaching background in the IB specifically — whether they have completed IB teacher training, taught at an IB World School, studied the IB curriculum themselves, or have a sustained track record of supporting IB students. We look at subject knowledge, programme familiarity, and communication clarity. Once tutoring begins, we encourage an open communication structure: parents should receive regular updates on what topics are being covered, where the student is in their IA or revision timeline, and whether the pace needs adjustment. Good tutoring at the IB level is transparent — a tutor who keeps a parent informed tends to be one who has a genuine handle on the student's progress. If something is not working — scheduling, fit, pace, or approach — families can raise it with IB Gram directly so that adjustments can be made. The goal is a working relationship, not a one-off transaction.
items: Tutors reviewed for IB-specific teaching and academic background
Subject familiarity and programme knowledge assessed before listing
Parents encouraged to request regular progress updates from tutors
Concerns about fit or approach can be raised with IB Gram directly

contentBlocks.heading: School Context in the Surajkund and Faridabad Corridor
contentBlocks.body: The Surajkund area sits within a broader school corridor that includes the Green Fields sector, the central Faridabad clusters, and communities in Neharpar and Greater Faridabad. Families in this corridor tend to enroll children in schools that range from established CBSE institutions to those that have adopted international curricula, including the IB. Students who move between curriculum systems — for example, a family relocating from an IB school in Gurugram or Delhi to Faridabad — sometimes find that local tutoring support for the IB is less dense than in larger metro hubs. This is precisely where IB Gram's hybrid and online matching is useful, as it extends the tutor pool beyond purely local options. IB Gram is an independent tutoring platform and is not officially affiliated with any school mentioned in connection with this area unless specifically stated. We do not represent any school, and parents should verify curriculum and programme details directly with their child's institution. Our role is to connect students with subject tutors who understand the IB framework, not to advise on school selection or school-level decisions.
items: Families from Green Fields, Neharpar and Greater Faridabad school communities
Students relocating from IB schools in Gurugram or Delhi benefit from online matching
IB Gram is independent of all schools in the Faridabad area
School-specific curriculum details should be confirmed directly with the institution

contentBlocks.heading: Honest Notes on Availability and Academic Integrity
contentBlocks.body: IB Gram aims to be straightforward with families about two things that matter. First, availability: finding the right IB tutor for a specific subject, level, location, and schedule in Surajkund is not always instant. Availability depends on subject, schedule, exact location, tutor fit, and mode. Niche combinations — for example, a home tutor for Math AA HL who can travel to Surajkund on weekday evenings — may take longer to source than a general online tutor for a common DP subject. We will tell you honestly what we can match rather than overpromise. Second, academic integrity: IB tutors play a supporting role. They explain concepts, work through practice problems, give feedback on draft structure, and help students understand the criteria they are being assessed against. They do not write Internal Assessments, Extended Essays, or TOK work for students, and any tutor who offers to do so is not aligned with IB academic honesty policy. A good tutor makes the student think harder and more clearly — the assessed work must remain the student's own. IB Gram supports this boundary and encourages parents to reinforce it at home as well.
items: Availability varies by subject, level, location, schedule and tutor fit
Niche home-tutor requests may take longer to match than online sessions
Tutors guide thinking and structure; assessed work stays student-authored
IB academic integrity policy applies to IA, EE and TOK components

contentBlocks.heading: Getting Started with IB Tutoring near Surajkund
contentBlocks.body: If your child is navigating the IB programme from the Surajkund area — whether they are in PYP, MYP, or the DP years — the clearest first step is a free academic consultation with IB Gram. This is a structured conversation, not a sales call. We ask about the programme and year group, the specific subjects and levels involved, the school's upcoming internal deadlines, and what mode of tutoring works for your family logistically. Based on that, we identify potential tutor matches from our reviewed pool, including tutors who can travel to Surajkund or nearby areas, tutors available online, or both. If you are in DP2 and Internal Assessment deadlines are approaching, we try to move quickly. If you are in MYP or PYP and the need is less urgent, we take a bit more time to find the right fit rather than the fastest one. Either way, the process starts with honest information, not promises about grades or outcomes. IB tutoring done well is about building the student's own capability — and that is what IB Gram is here to support.
items: Free consultation covers programme, subjects, levels and school deadlines
Tutor options include home visits, online sessions and hybrid arrangements
DP2 students with upcoming IA deadlines given priority matching
Process focuses on fit and genuine academic need, not guaranteed results

faqs.question: Can a tutor actually travel to Surajkund for home sessions?
faqs.answer: Yes, home visits to Surajkund are possible. Tutors based in nearby Faridabad sectors such as Sector 15, 16, 21, and 28, as well as those in Green Fields and Neharpar, can often travel to Surajkund within a reasonable commute. Tutors from South-East Delhi — Badarpur or Tughlagabad — can also reach Surajkund in off-peak hours via Shooting Range Road. That said, peak-hour traffic on this corridor can be significant, so scheduling matters. If consistent home visits are essential, IB Gram will confirm travel feasibility with the tutor before finalising any match. For families where timing is tight, hybrid or fully online sessions are a reliable alternative.

faqs.question: Which IB subjects are most commonly requested by students in the Faridabad area?
faqs.answer: Among DP students in the Faridabad and Surajkund area, the most frequent requests are for Mathematics — both Math AA HL and Math AI HL — followed by Physics HL, Chemistry HL, and Economics. Biology HL comes up regularly from students considering medicine-related degree pathways. English A support is also requested, particularly for the Individual Oral and DP literary analysis essays. MYP students most often need help in Mathematics and Sciences, where the criterion-based assessment requires a different kind of feedback than most students are used to. Availability for specific subjects and levels varies, and IB Gram will confirm what is matchable at the time of consultation.

faqs.question: My child is in MYP, not DP. Does IB Gram help with MYP as well?
faqs.answer: Yes, IB Gram supports students in all three IB programmes — PYP, MYP, and DP. MYP tutoring requires familiarity with the specific assessment criteria used in each subject area, such as Criterion A through D in Mathematics or the strands in Language and Literature. A tutor who only knows CBSE or ICSE marking schemes may not understand why a technically correct answer receives a lower score against MYP criteria. IB Gram looks specifically for tutors with MYP experience when matching families in that programme, and the same structured consultation process applies regardless of which IB year group your child is in.

faqs.question: How far in advance should we contact IB Gram before DP Internal Assessment deadlines?
faqs.answer: The earlier the better, but realistically, many families contact us when a deadline is already close. If your school's IA submission window is two to three months away, that is a workable timeline for most subjects. If the deadline is within a few weeks, we will try to prioritise your match and be honest about what is feasible in the remaining time. For the Extended Essay and TOK, which tend to have earlier school-set deadlines than students expect, starting in DP1 or early DP2 is strongly advisable. Tutors can help a student understand EE topic selection, research structure, and TOK argument framing well before the actual writing phase — and that early support tends to produce more coherent final submissions.

faqs.question: Is online tutoring genuinely effective for IB subjects, or is in-person better?
faqs.answer: For most IB subjects at the DP level, online tutoring is highly effective and is the preferred mode for many experienced IB tutors. DP work is heavily document-based — IA drafts, commentary outlines, practice paper solutions — and these translate well to screen-sharing and collaborative tools. The consistency of online sessions also benefits DP2 students whose school timetable and IA deadlines shift week to week; an online tutor is not subject to traffic or travel cancellations. In-person sessions tend to be preferred for younger PYP and MYP students who engage better with physical materials and face-to-face interaction. For families near Surajkund, online access to tutors across Delhi-NCR also broadens the available pool significantly beyond what a purely local search would yield.

faqs.question: Is IB Gram officially connected to our school or to the IB Organisation?
faqs.answer: No. IB Gram is an independent tutoring platform and is not officially affiliated with any school or with the International Baccalaureate Organisation unless specifically stated. We do not represent schools in Faridabad, the NCR, or anywhere else, and we do not have any formal connection to the IB Organisation, Cambridge, Pearson Edexcel, or any other examining body. Tutors listed on IB Gram are independent educators, not representatives of any institution. Parents should verify programme and curriculum details directly with their child's school. Our role is to help match students with qualified, experienced IB tutors — that is the full scope of what we do.

finalCta: Book a free academic consultation with IB Gram to discuss your child's IB programme, subjects, and schedule. We will identify realistic tutor options for Surajkund — whether home visits, online sessions, or a hybrid arrangement — and be straightforward about what is available. No grade guarantees, no pressure: just an honest conversation about how we can help.
